back: MVCC is a concurrency control method that maintains multiple versions of data items simultaneously, allowing read operations to access historical data without blocking writes and vice versa.

front: What is a snapshot in MVCC?
back: A snapshot is a consistent view of the database at a specific point in time. All reads within a transaction see data as it existed at the start of the transaction.

front: What is the difference between dirty read and non-repeatable read?
back: Dirty read reads uncommitted data from other transactions. Non-repeatable read occurs when the same query returns different results within the same transaction due to concurrent updates.

front: What is write-ahead logging (WAL) in MVCC?
back: WAL ensures that transaction logs are written to stable storage before the actual data pages are updated, ensuring durability and recovery capabilities.

front: What is timestamp ordering (T/O) in MVCC?
back: A concurrency control technique that assigns unique timestamps to transactions and uses these timestamps to determine the order of operations and validate serializability.
A isolation level where a transaction sees all committed changes made before it starts, but none of the changes made by concurrent uncommitted transactions.

front: What is a version chain in MVCC?
back: A linked list structure that connects multiple versions of the same data item, allowing the system to traverse from the latest version to older historical versions.

front: What is the purpose of Commit Timestamp in MVCC?
back: Commit timestamp marks when a transaction successfully completes, enabling the system to determine which version of data is visible to other transactions.

front: What is rollback segment in MVCC?
back: A storage area that maintains old versions of data items modified by transactions, used to reconstruct previous states when transactions abort.

front: What is the difference between MVCC and locking-based concurrency control?
back: Locking blocks readers when writers hold locks, whereas MVCC allows concurrent reads without blocking by serving snapshot versions.

front: What is a garbage collection in MVCC context?
back: The process of identifying and removing obsolete or outdated data versions that are no longer needed by any active transaction.

front: What is phantom read in MVCC?
back: A phenomenon where a transaction re-executes a query and gets a different set of rows due to insertions/deletions by concurrent transactions.

front: What is Thomas Write Rule in MVCC?
back: A modification to timestamp ordering that allows certain out-of-order writes to be ignored rather than rejected, improving concurrency.

front: What is read consistency in MVCC?
back: The guarantee that all queries within a transaction see data from the same point in time, providing a consistent database state view.

front: What is the role of transaction ID in MVCC?
back: A unique identifier assigned to each transaction, used to determine version visibility and enforce serialization order.
